projects.title: sim-arm
projects.detail: A from-scratch simulator for articulated robot arms, integrating rigid-body dynamics, contact resolution, and joint limits in a configurable control loop. Built to test motion-planning and control ideas without risking real hardware. Every force, constraint, and integration step is inspectable and logged.

projects.title: kalman-rs
projects.detail: A small, well-typed Kalman/EKF/UKF library focused on legibility and zero-allocation hot paths. Implements common Bayesian filters with an emphasis on numerical stability and ergonomic APIs.

projects.title: nbody-gpu
projects.detail: A tiled, shared-memory CUDA implementation of the all-pairs n-body problem capable of pushing millions of particles per second. Built as a robotics club project to explore GPU programming and numerical methods. Barnes-Hut acceleration is a planned follow-up.
